headline: The Great AI Reality Check: Companies Moving from Pilots to Payoff

stories.title: AI Transformation's "Last Mile" Problem
stories.summary: Companies are hitting a wall converting AI pilots into real business value—it's not about better tech, it's about redesigning ops and culture.
stories.takeaway: 88% of orgs use AI in some function, but only 1-in-10 deployed agents past pilot. The gap between experimentation and actual impact is widening.
stories.strategyTake: Stop building disconnected pilots. Scale AI through enterprise-wide programs with real ops redesign, not just tool rollouts.

stories.title: Scaling AI Is Actually About Redesigning Your Entire Workforce
stories.summary: BCG's analysis shows companies winning at AI aren't just deploying tech—they're fundamentally restructuring how teams work with AI as collaborators.
stories.takeaway: CHROs are becoming architects of "hybrid workforces," redesigning roles so humans + AI agents work together. It's an org restructure, not an IT project.
stories.strategyTake: Treat AI adoption like you'd treat a merger. Redefine roles, redesign workflows, and invest heavily in talent development. Culture matters more than models.

stories.title: JPMorgan Chase Reclassifies AI From "R&D" to "Core Infrastructure"
stories.summary: JPMorgan's pivoting its entire playbook—moving AI from experimental pilot spending to mission-critical infrastructure with measurable bottom-line impact.
stories.takeaway: This signals a seismic shift: AI is no longer optional or experimental. Banks embedding it into core ops (productivity, security, personalization) see real ROI.
stories.strategyTake: The winners are treating AI like infrastructure, not tools. Move budget from "try it" to "build it at scale" with governance and accountability baked in.

stories.title: Why AI Adoption Failures Hit When Companies Automate Broken Processes
stories.summary: Gartner predicts 40% of agentic AI projects will fail by 2027—not because tech doesn't work, but because orgs are automating broken ops instead of redesigning them first.
stories.takeaway: The trap: Taking your messy, human-centered process and just "automating" it with agents. Real wins come from rethinking the process itself.
stories.strategyTake: Before automating anything, redesign it. Ask "How should this work if AI could do any part of it?" not "How do I automate what we do today?"

watchNext.title: Microsoft's AI Transformation Hitting the Wall: A $357B Valuation Correction
watchNext.why: Microsoft's facing a major test—Azure growth slowed, and investors questioned whether massive CapEx translates to actual revenue. Worth watching how they pivot from infrastructure to monetization.

watchNext.title: Morgan Stanley: A Massive AI Breakthrough Is Coming H1 2026—and Most Orgs Aren't Ready
watchNext.why: Morgan Stanley's warning about "transformative AI" imminent. Lowkey this could shift everything—if they're right about 10x compute = 2x intelligence, the landscape changes fast.
